title: Racosperma improcerum
taxonomy: Racosperma improcerum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a part of the Acacia genus, which is a group of trees and shrubs commonly known as acacia trees. Racosperma improcerum was previously classified as Acacia improcera, but it was reclassified into the Racosperma genus. The Racosperma genus is a group of plants that were previously classified as Acacia, but were later reclassified due to molecular and morphological differences. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of Racosperma improcerum.

parts: Racosperma improcerum, commonly known as the Red Hill Wattle, has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine. intro:

Racosperma, or Acacia, is a genus of shrubs and trees in the family Fabaceae, native to Australia and surrounding islands. The plant contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its health benefits. The health benefits of Racosperma include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. 

The most common herbal preparations made from Racosperma include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
